body.title: CLOSED: Replace tmux-capture console.log with FileHandler subscriber
body.result: DailyConsoleFileHandler in logging_setup.py; pipe-pane removed from igor launcher; rotate_igor_console.py deleted; 4478 tests pass
body.description:
Currently ~/.agent_datacenter/logs/Igor-wild-0001/YYYYMMDD.console.log is produced by a tmux pipe-pane capture (managed by rotate_igor_console.py). This captures raw terminal output including non-logging sequences like [?2004l (bracketed paste mode). Replace with a Python FileHandler subscribed to the same senders/levels as the console StreamHandler, using the same color formatter — both outputs come from the logging pipeline, terminal artifacts never reach the file. Remove the tmux pipe-pane capture mechanism and the rotate_igor_console.py cron entry.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/rotate_igor_console.py, agent_datacenter/logcontrol/control_center.py, wild_igor/igor/logging_setup.py (TBD — discovery step in sprint), igor bash launch script (pipe-pane removal)
**Design rules:** logging-centralization — LoggingControlCenter is the authority for file handlers
**Scope boundary:** In scope: add FileHandler to logging init with same color formatter as StreamHandler; remove tmux pipe-pane capture and cron entry. Out of scope: changing log levels, adding new senders, modifying log format.
**Test plan:** After change, start Igor fresh — verify YYYYMMDD.console.log contains ANSI color codes, contains no [?2004l, and content matches console output. Run existing logging tests.
**Pass condition:** Fresh Igor start produces a .console.log that Kate renders with colors and contains no terminal control sequences outside SGR (color) codes.
